visual_findings.label: Facial expression
visual_findings.summary: Neutral to slightly tired expression; minimal emotional reactivity observed.
visual_findings.evidence: Patient maintains steady eye contact with camera; facial muscles show no overt signs of distress, confusion, or emotional flattening. Occasional hand-to-head gesture may suggest fatigue or concentration.

visual_findings.label: Gaze and attention
visual_findings.summary: Consistent visual focus on camera; no signs of disengagement or wandering gaze.
visual_findings.evidence: Patient’s eyes remain directed toward the camera throughout the clip, indicating sustained attention to the interaction.

body_findings.label: Hand-to-head gesture
body_findings.summary: Repetitive hand-to-head gesture observed, possibly indicating fatigue, concentration, or mild discomfort.
body_findings.evidence: Patient places hand on forehead and temple area multiple times during the clip, consistent with a self-soothing or cognitive load behavior.

voice_findings.label: Speech clarity
voice_findings.summary: Speech is clear and articulate; no signs of slurring, hesitations, or disorganized syntax.
voice_findings.evidence: Patient speaks in a steady, fluent manner with appropriate pacing and intonation. No phonological or prosodic abnormalities detected.

content_findings.label: Self-report of activity
content_findings.summary: Patient reports working from home, writing code, and handling details — no self-reported cognitive difficulties.
content_findings.evidence: Patient states: 'I'm now working at home... I'm now writing codes and dealing with the details...'

screening_summary: The patient demonstrates clear speech, consistent visual attention, and no overt signs of cognitive impairment or emotional dysregulation. The hand-to-head gesture may reflect fatigue or concentration but is not diagnostic. No self-reported concerns or caregiver input are present. No persuasive evidence of dementia-like symptoms is observed.
evidence_against_risk: Fluent, coherent speech with no disorganization or word-finding difficulty.
Steady eye contact and visual engagement with the camera.
No signs of memory loss, disorientation, or functional impairment reported or observed.
alternative_explanations: Hand-to-head gesture may reflect work-related fatigue or concentration rather than cognitive decline.
Neutral affect may reflect a calm or reserved personality rather than apathy or emotional flattening.
emotional_assessment_findings: Neutral affect with minimal emotional expression; no overt signs of anxiety, frustration, or apathy.
Hand-to-head gesture may reflect mild cognitive load or fatigue, but not distress or confusion.
risk_control_suggestions: Continue routine monitoring if part of a longitudinal study.
No immediate intervention or diagnostic follow-up is warranted based on this clip alone.
visit_recommendation: Routine follow-up or no further action required at this time.
future_risk_trend_summary: Insufficient data to predict future cognitive decline from a single clip. Longitudinal assessment is recommended if clinical concern persists.
context_notes: This is a single-clinic screening clip; not diagnostic.
No caregiver or interviewer concerns are expressed in the clip.
disclaimer: Research demo only. This output is not a diagnosis and must not be used as a standalone medical decision.
